Problem Identified: Hardcoded credentials (etl_user, etl_pass123) in plain text.
Production Impact: High vulnerability risk; credentials exposed in version control (Git). OJK audit failure
Proposed Fix: Inject credentials dynamically using AWS Secrets Manager and retrieve them via Spark secrets or orchestrator environment context.

Problem Identified: Single-threaded JDBC read on ACCOUNTS and TRANSACTIONS (no partitioning options)
Production Impact: Spark opens only 1 connection, pulling millions of rows sequentially. Maximizes execution time (4+ hours).
Proposed Fix: Use JDBC concurrent partitioning options: specify partitionColumn, lowerBound, upperBound, and numPartitions or even use predicates if no good column to partition on (has to be numeric) to pull data in parallel.

Problem Identified: Ineffective Predicate Pushdown due to Type Mismatch (txn_date >= "2026-01-01").
Production Impact: Spark cannot push down the string literal filter to Oracle. Forces network overload by streaming 60M+ historical rows into Spark memory.
Proposed Fix: Cast the filter explicitly using proper timestamp types or pass a pre-filtered query directly into the JDBC dbtable option.

Production Impact: The pipeline calculates the same static snapshot indefinitely instead of capturing daily delta changes.
Proposed Fix: Parametrization: pass the dynamic processing date (e.g., logical execution date from Airflow or metadata table on Databricks) as a system argument.

Problem Identified: Dangerous .mode("overwrite") directly on production Parquet files.
Production Impact: Mid-write job failures corrupt or wipe out the existing production data layer, causing downstream breaks.
Proposed Fix: Implement Delta Lake / ACID Compliant storage format. Alternatively, use a staging-to-final directory swap mechanism with data validation.

Problem Identified: Unoptimized groupBy on low-cardinality and descriptive columns (customer_id, account_type, balance) followed by broad aggregation.
Production Impact: Forces a massive Spark shuffle across the cluster network, killing performance for 8M+ records.
Proposed Fix: Turn on Adaptive Query Execution (AQE), ensure columns match upstream partition keys, or aggregate transaction data independently before joining to the master account table.
